source: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.178 + ANSI/ITSDF B56.1 Safety Standard for Low Lift and High Lift Trucks
topic: Forklift operation clearances, turning radii, and aisle requirements

facts.context: Typical counterbalance forklift body width (2-3 ton class)
Overall length including forks extended — used for aisle turn calculation
Minimum turning radius for standard 2-ton counterbalance forklift (inside radius)
Operating turning radius including load swing — use this for layout clearance
ANSI B56.1 — minimum aisle width to execute a 90-degree turn with standard load
Straight one-way forklift travel with no turning — minimum clear width
Two-way forklift traffic — two vehicle widths + 0.6m clearance each side
OSHA 1910.178(g) — dedicated pedestrian lane min 6 ft when co-located with forklift traffic
Typical indoor speed limit — affects stopping distance and safety zone radius
Approximate stopping distance at 8km/h with full load — clear zone ahead of machine
Minimum side clearance between forklift path and fixed wall or structure
Minimum clearance between forklift operating zone and fixed machinery — OSHA general
Depth of loading/staging zone for forklift to maneuver with loaded pallet (20 ft)
Extra clearance at corridor intersections and blind corners for forklift approach
Minimum ceiling/beam clearance when forklift mast is fully raised (3-ton class)
Minimum clear zone around forklift battery charging stations — ventilation + access
